{{Short description|American professor and retired soccer player}}

| birth_place       = United States

Clemson University, 2008–2016

[[University of Washington]] ([[Master's degree|MS]])
[[Pennsylvania State University]] ([[Doctor of Philosophy|PhD]])
| doctoral_students = 
| awards            = National Science Foundation, CAREER
National Science Foundation, Interdisciplinary Science and Education (INSPIRE)
Nerdscholar, 40 under 40
| website           = https://www.leidyklotz.com/ 
}}

'''Leidy Klotz''' (born July 14, 1978) is an American scientist and author who studies and writes about design and problem-solving. He is a professor of engineering and architecture at the [[University of Virginia]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.leidyklotz.com/about-leidy|title = About Leidy}}</ref> Klotz has published in scientific journals including ''[[Nature (journal)|Nature]]''<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Adams |first=Gabrielle S. |last2=Converse |first2=Benjamin A. |last3=Hales |first3=Andrew H. |last4=Klotz |first4=Leidy E. |date=April 2021 |title=People systematically overlook subtractive changes |url=https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-021-03380-y |journal=Nature |language=en |volume=592 |issue=7853 |pages=258–261 |doi=10.1038/s41586-021-03380-y |issn=1476-4687|url-access=subscription }}</ref> and ''[[Science Advances]]''<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Hazari |first=Zahra |last2=Potvin |first2=Geoff |last3=Cribbs |first3=Jennifer D. |last4=Godwin |first4=Allison |last5=Scott |first5=Tyler D. |last6=Klotz |first6=Leidy |date=2017-08-09 |title=Interest in STEM is contagious for students in biology, chemistry, and physics classes |url=https://www.science.org/doi/full/10.1126/sciadv.1700046 |journal=Science Advances |volume=3 |issue=8 |article-number=e1700046 |doi=10.1126/sciadv.1700046 |pmc=5550226 |pmid=28808678}}</ref> and in other publications such as ''[[The Washington Post]]'', ''[[Harvard Business Review]]'', ''[[Fast Company]]'', and ''[[The Globe and Mail]]''. He is also the author of two popular books: ''Subtract: The Untapped Science of Less'' (2021), which discusses design and problem-solving, and ''Sustainability through Soccer'' (2016), a work about [[systems thinking]].

Klotz is a retired [[United Soccer League]] soccer player.<ref>[https://cortlandstandard.net/2016/08/02/former-trojans-booter-klotz-a-1st-time-author/ Former Trojans booter Klotz a 1st-time author] cortlandstandard.net</ref>

== Soccer career ==
{{Infobox football biography
| name = Leidy Klotz
| position = [[midfielder]]
| years1 = 2000–2001
| clubs1 = [[Pittsburgh Riverhounds]]<ref>[https://news.virginia.edu/content/uva-professor-teaches-sustainability-through-soccer UVA Professor Teaches Sustainability Through Soccer] news.virginia.edu</ref>
| caps1 = 31
| goals1 = 3
| youthclubs1 = Lafayette College
| youthyears1 = 1996–1999
}}
Klotz played professionally for the [[Pittsburgh Riverhounds SC|Pittsburgh Riverhounds]],<ref>{{Cite web|title=Riverhounds miss late penalty kick, settle for 1–1 tie with Toronto|url=https://old.post-gazette.com/sports_headlines/20000430hounds3.asp|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220221172050/https://old.post-gazette.com/sports_headlines/20000430hounds3.asp|archive-date=February 21, 2022|access-date=2022-02-21|website=old.post-gazette.com}}</ref> making 31 appearances and scoring 3 goals in the 2000 and 2001 seasons. Klotz was a 2x Division I All-American at Lafayette College.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Klotz Making Mark In Professional Soccer Ranks|url=https://goleopards.com/news/2000/5/18/klotz_making_mark_in_professional_soccer_ranks.aspx|access-date=2022-02-21|website=Lafayette College Athletics|date=18 May 2000 |language=en}}</ref> He was inducted into the Lafayette College Hall of Fame in 2016<ref>{{Cite web|title=maroonclub mtt leidy klotz 1033091 html|url=https://goleopards.com/sports/2018/9/17/_maroonclub_mtt_leidy_klotz_1033091_html.aspx|access-date=2022-02-21|website=Lafayette College Athletics|language=en}}</ref> and the Homer High School Hall of Fame in 2009.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Athletics Home / Hall of Fame|url=https://www.homercentral.org/Page/http%3A%2F%2Fwww.homercentral.org%2Fsite%2Fdefault.aspx%3FPageID%3D112|access-date=2022-02-21|website=www.homercentral.org|language=en}}</ref>

== Scientific career ==
Klotz is currently a professor of Engineering<ref>{{Cite web|last1=Systems|first1=Leidy Klotz Engineering|last2=Charlottesville|first2=Environment Office: Olsson Hall 111B PO Box 400747|date=2017-04-06|title=Leidy Klotz|url=https://engineering.virginia.edu/faculty/leidy-klotz|access-date=2022-02-21|website=University of Virginia School of Engineering and Applied Science|language=en}}</ref> and Architecture<ref>{{Cite web|title=LEIDY KLOTZ|url=http://www.arch.virginia.edu/people/leidy-klotz|access-date=2022-02-21|website=University of Virginia School of Architecture|language=EN-US}}</ref> at the [[University of Virginia]]. He studies and teaches the science of design, and, in 2019, co-chaired a ''[[List of Nature Research journals|Nature Sustainability]]'' Expert Panel on this topic.<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Klotz|first1=Leidy|last2=Pickering|first2=John|last3=Schmidt|first3=Ruth|last4=Weber|first4=Elke U.|date=December 2019|title=Design behaviour for sustainability|url=https://www.nature.com/articles/s41893-019-0449-1|journal=Nature Sustainability|language=en|volume=2|issue=12|pages=1067–1069|doi=10.1038/s41893-019-0449-1|bibcode=2019NatSu...2.1067K |s2cid=209168774|issn=2398-9629|url-access=subscription}}</ref> He has published articles in ''Nature''<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Adams|first1=Gabrielle S.|last2=Converse|first2=Benjamin A.|last3=Hales|first3=Andrew H.|last4=Klotz|first4=Leidy E.|date=April 2021|title=People systematically overlook subtractive changes|url=https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-021-03380-y|journal=Nature|language=en|volume=592|issue=7853|pages=258–261|doi=10.1038/s41586-021-03380-y|pmid=33828317|bibcode=2021Natur.592..258A|s2cid=233185662|issn=1476-4687|url-access=subscription}}</ref> and ''Science''<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Hazari|first1=Zahra|last2=Potvin|first2=Geoff|last3=Cribbs|first3=Jennifer D.|last4=Godwin|first4=Allison|last5=Scott|first5=Tyler D.|last6=Klotz|first6=Leidy|date=August 2017|title=Interest in STEM is contagious for students in biology, chemistry, and physics classes|journal=Science Advances|volume=3|issue=8|article-number=e1700046|language=EN|doi=10.1126/sciadv.1700046|pmc=5550226|pmid=28808678|bibcode=2017SciA....3E0046H}}</ref> and, as of 2022, has an [[h-index]] of 24.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Leidy Klotz|url=https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=BwxRqBMAAAAJ&hl=en|access-date=2021-08-13|website=scholar.google.com}}</ref> His research has been covered by ''The Washington Post'',<ref>{{Cite news|title=Analysis {{!}} Humans solve problems by adding complexity, even when it's against our best interests|language=en-US|newspaper=Washington Post|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/business/2021/04/16/bias-problem-solving-nature/|access-date=2022-02-21|issn=0190-8286}}</ref> ''The Wall Street Journal'',<ref>{{Cite news|last=Zweig|first=Jason|date=2021-04-23|title=How to Keep Your Cool When Markets Are Sizzling|language=en-US|work=Wall Street Journal|url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/how-to-keep-your-cool-when-markets-are-sizzling-11619190301|access-date=2022-02-21|issn=0099-9660}}</ref> ''The World Economic Forum'',<ref>{{Cite web|title=This Lego experiment shows our brains prefer adding. Here's why it matters|url=https://www.weforum.org/agenda/2021/04/brains-prefer-adding-sustainability/|access-date=2022-02-21|website=World Economic Forum|language=en}}</ref> ''Grist'',<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-04-16|title=Goodbye, old freeways? How subtraction could address climate change|url=https://grist.org/science/subtraction-climate-change-freeways-problem-solving/|access-date=2022-02-21|website=Grist|language=en-us}}</ref> ''Scientific American'',<ref>{{Cite web|last=Kwon|first=Diana|title=Our Brain Typically Overlooks This Brilliant Problem-Solving Strategy|url=https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/our-brain-typically-overlooks-this-brilliant-problem-solving-strategy/|access-date=2022-02-21|website=Scientific American|date=July 2021 |language=en}}</ref> and newspapers around the world.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Altmetric – People systematically overlook subtractive changes|url=https://nature.altmetric.com/details/103451152/news|access-date=2022-02-21|website=nature.altmetric.com}}</ref>

=== Books ===
''IN A GOOD PLACE: How the Spaces Where We Live, Work, and Play Can Help Us Thrive'' (Little, Brown, 2026).<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://www.hachettebookgroup.com/titles/leidy-klotz-phd/in-a-good-place/9780316567367/ |title=In a Good Place |date=2025-08-04 |isbn=978-0-316-56736-7 |language=en-US}}</ref> ''In a Good Place'' draws on evidence across psychology, architecture, and more to show how deliberate engagement with physical spaces can reliably enhance well-being. The book argues that physical environments are one of the best ways to meet basic psychological needs for agency, competence, and connection.

''SUBTRACT: The Untapped Science of Less'' (Flatiron Books, 2021).<ref>{{Cite web|title=Subtract {{!}} Leidy Klotz {{!}} Macmillan|url=https://us.macmillan.com/books/9781250249876/subtract/|access-date=2025-04-22|website=US Macmillan|language=en-US}}</ref> ''Subtract'' has received various accolades.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-12-09|title=Behavioral Scientist's Notable Books of 2021 – By Antonia Violante, Heather Graci, & Evan Nesterak|url=https://behavioralscientist.org/behavioral-scientists-notable-books-of-2021/|access-date=2022-02-22|website=Behavioral Scientist|language=en}}</ref><sup>,</sup><ref>{{Cite web|title=WDO {{!}} Resources {{!}} Recommended Readings|url=https://wdo.org/resources/recommended-readings/|access-date=2022-02-22|language=en-US}}</ref><sup>,</sup><ref>{{Cite web|title=Rare Books: Summer 2021|url=https://rare.org/blog/rare-books-summer-2021/|access-date=2022-02-22|website=Rare|language=en-US}}</ref><sup>,</sup><ref>{{Cite web |last=Editors |first=MIT SMR |title=Eight Business Books to Challenge Your Thinking |url=https://sloanreview.mit.edu/article/eight-business-books-to-challenge-your-thinking/ |access-date=2022-08-11 |website=MIT Sloan Management Review |date=7 July 2022 |language=en-US}}</ref><sup>,</sup><ref>{{Cite web |last=Stulberg |first=Brad |date=2021-12-02 |title=The 21 Best Books of 2021 for Sustainable Success and Well-Being |url=https://thegrowtheq.com/the-21-best-books-of-2021-for-sustainable-success-and-well-being/ |access-date=2022-08-11 |website=The Growth Equation |language=en-US}}</ref><sup>,</sup><ref>{{Cite web|title=School improvement: why less is more|url=https://www.tes.com/magazine/teaching-learning/general/school-improvement-why-less-more|access-date=2022-02-22|website=Tes Magazine|language=en}}</ref> The book builds from Klotz's research showing that the human mind tends to add before taking away, even to our detriment. The first half of the book discusses biological, cultural, and socio-economic explanations for this tendency. The second half of the book offers ways to overcome harmful subtraction neglect, drawing from science and from subtracting examples and exemplars such as: [[Maya Lin]], [[Elinor Ostrom]], The [[California State Route 480|Embarcadero Freeway]], [[Balance bicycle|Balance Bikes]], [[Anna Keichline]], and [[Bruce Springsteen]]. 

He also wrote ''Sustainability through Soccer'' with University of California Press in  2016.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Klotz|first=Leidy|url=https://www.ucpress.edu/book/9780520287815/sustainability-through-soccer|title=Sustainability through Soccer: An Unexpected Approach to Saving Our World|date=July 2016|publisher=Univ of California Press |isbn=978-0-520-28781-5|language=en}}</ref>

=== Notable research articles ===

* "People Systematically Overlook Subtractive Changes."<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Adams|first1=Gabrielle S.|last2=Converse|first2=Benjamin A.|last3=Hales|first3=Andrew H.|last4=Klotz|first4=Leidy E.|date=April 2021|title=People systematically overlook subtractive changes|url=https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-021-03380-y|journal=Nature|language=en|volume=592|issue=7853|pages=258–261|doi=10.1038/s41586-021-03380-y|pmid=33828317|bibcode=2021Natur.592..258A|s2cid=233185662|issn=1476-4687|url-access=subscription}}</ref> Nature. 592. 258–261. Adams, G., Converse, B., Hales, A., and Klotz, L. (2021)
* "Embracing Behavioral Science."<ref>{{Cite web|title=Embracing Behavioral Science|url=https://nae.edu/244757/Embracing-Behavioral-Science|access-date=2022-02-22|website=NAE Website}}</ref> The Bridge – National Academy of Engineering. 50 (4). Klotz, L., and Pickering, J. (2020)
* "Beyond rationality in engineering design for sustainability."<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Klotz|first1=Leidy|last2=Weber|first2=Elke|last3=Johnson|first3=Eric|last4=Shealy|first4=Tripp|last5=Hernandez|first5=Morela|last6=Gordon|first6=Bethany|date=May 2018|title=Beyond rationality in engineering design for sustainability|url=https://www.nature.com/articles/s41893-018-0054-8|journal=Nature Sustainability|language=en|volume=1|issue=5|pages=225–233|doi=10.1038/s41893-018-0054-8|bibcode=2018NatSu...1..225K |s2cid=158122486|issn=2398-9629|url-access=subscription}}</ref> Nature Sustainability. 1: 225–233. Klotz, L., Weber, E., Johnson, E., Shealy, E., Hernandez, M., and Gordon, B. (2018)
* "Sustainability as a route to broaden participation in engineering."<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Klotz|first1=Leidy|last2=Potvin|first2=Geoff|last3=Godwin|first3=Allison|last4=Cribbs|first4=Jennifer|last5=Hazari|first5=Zahra|last6=Barclay|first6=Nicole|date=2014|title=Sustainability as a Route to Broadening Participation in Engineering|url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1002/jee.20034|journal=Journal of Engineering Education|language=en|volume=103|issue=1|pages=137–153|doi=10.1002/jee.20034|s2cid=108818733|issn=2168-9830|url-access=subscription}}</ref> Journal of Engineering Education, 103(1): 137–153. Klotz, L., et al. (2014).
* "A framework for sustainable whole systems design."<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Blizzard|first1=Jacqualyn L.|last2=Klotz|first2=Leidy E.|date=2012-09-01|title=A framework for sustainable whole systems design|url=https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0142694X12000105|journal=Design Studies|language=en|volume=33|issue=5|pages=456–479|doi=10.1016/j.destud.2012.03.001|issn=0142-694X|url-access=subscription}}</ref> Design Studies. 33(5) 456–479. Blizzard, J., and Klotz, L. (2012).

=== Science communication ===
Klotz writes about research and design for popular outlets such as ''The Washington Post'',<ref>{{Cite news|title=Perspective {{!}} We instinctively add on new features and fixes. Why don't we subtract instead?|language=en-US|newspaper=Washington Post|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/outlook/2021/04/15/psychology-innovation-subtraction-addition/|access-date=2022-02-22|issn=0190-8286}}</ref> ''Fast Company'',<ref>{{Cite web|last=Klotz|first=Leidy|date=2018-06-06|title=The little-known behavioral scientist who transformed cities all over the world|url=https://www.fastcompany.com/90174746/the-little-known-behavioral-scientist-who-transformed-cities-all-over-the-world|access-date=2022-02-22|website=Fast Company|language=en-US}}</ref> and ''Harvard Business Review''.<ref>{{Cite news|last1=Adams|first1=Gabrielle|last2=Converse|first2=Benjamin A.|last3=Hales|first3=Andrew|last4=Klotz|first4=Leidy|date=2022-02-04|title=When Subtraction Adds Value|work=Harvard Business Review|url=https://hbr.org/2022/02/when-subtraction-adds-value|access-date=2022-02-22|issn=0017-8012}}</ref>

Klotz frequently appears on top science podcasts and radio shows such as [[Freakonomics Radio]],<ref>{{Cite web|last=Levey|first=Morgan|title=Leidy Klotz on Why the Best Solutions Involve Less — Not More|url=https://freakonomics.com/podcast/leidy-klotz-on-why-the-best-solutions-involve-less-not-more/|access-date=2022-02-21|website=Freakonomics|language=en}}</ref> [[Sean M. Carroll|Mindscape with Sean Carroll]],<ref>{{Cite web|title=162 {{!}} Leidy Klotz on Our Resistance to Subtractive Change – Sean Carroll|url=https://www.preposterousuniverse.com/podcast/2021/08/30/162-leidy-klotz-on-our-resistance-to-subtractive-change/comment-page-1/|access-date=2022-02-21|website=www.preposterousuniverse.com}}</ref> the [[Michael Shermer|Michael Shermer Show]],<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-09-18|title=210. Leidy Klotz on doing more with less, based on his book Subtract: The Untapped Science of Less|url=https://www.skeptic.com/michael-shermer-show/subtract-untapped-science-of-less-leidy-klotz/|access-date=2022-02-21|website=Skeptic|language=en}}</ref> and [[Public Radio International|PRI The World]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=People overlook power of subtraction, says engineer|url=https://theworld.org/stories/2021-06-02/people-overlook-power-subtraction-says-engineer|access-date=2022-02-21|website=The World from PRX|language=en}}</ref>
